message:
feat(tui): enhance BarChart on ToolMetrics with P99 sort and severity colors (br-333hh)

Sort latency distribution chart by P99 descending (worst latency first),
add severity-based P99 bar coloring (green < 100ms, yellow < 500ms,
red >= 500ms), and cap visible tools at 15. All 29 tool_metrics tests pass.

Closes br-333hh. Unblocks br-gk8vs, br-3jz52.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:

 {"id":"br-31zb9","title":"[track] T15: Comprehensive Testing & Quality Assurance","description":"Comprehensive test coverage for all new features added in Tracks 1-14.

TEST CATEGORIES:
1. Unit tests for data providers, adapters, form validators (50+ tests)
2. Snapshot tests for visual regression detection
3. Integration tests for chart rendering, tree building, export
4. E2E tests for clipboard, form submission, theme switching
5. Performance benchmarks for chart rendering, ambient effects, canvas

TESTING STRATEGY:
- Every track must have unit tests before merging
- Snapshot tests capture reference renders for each theme
- E2E tests run in headless mode via ftui-harness
- Performance tests enforce 16ms frame budget
- All tests run in CI with AM_TUI_EFFECTS=false and AM_TUI_AMBIENT=off","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] 50+ unit tests across all tracks
- [ ] Snapshot tests for all 5 themes
- [ ] E2E test script with 30+ assertions
- [ ] Performance benchmark enforcing 16ms budget
- [ ] All tests pass in CI

Plan-space hardening additions:
- [ ] Test and benchmark outputs include detailed structured diagnostics (scenario IDs, timings, error/reason codes, artifact paths, and repro commands)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:07:31.109806210Z","updated_at":"2026-02-14T04:33:04.410539373Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["quality","testing","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-31zb9","depends_on_id":"br-1xt0m","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:07:59.814240882Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":592,"issue_id":"br-31zb9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"TESTING STRATEGY (2026-02-13, RubyPrairie):

TEST CATEGORIES AND RATIONALE:

1. UNIT TESTS (T15.1, target 50+):
   - Chart data providers: verify aggregation math (buckets, percentiles, windowing)
   - Tree construction: verify parent-child building from flat message list
   - Log entry adapter: verify severity mapping and formatting
   - Mermaid generation: verify syntax output validity
   - Form validation: verify all field validators
   These are fast, deterministic, no dependencies on TUI rendering.

2. PERFORMANCE BENCHMARKS (T15.2):
   Critical for maintaining the 16ms frame budget. Benchmarks:
   - Dashboard with all widgets active (charts + effects + events)
   - Message list with 1000 items and markdown preview
   - Canvas heatmap with full data
   - Ambient effect rendering
   - Theme switch timing
   Use criterion for statistical benchmarks. These catch performance regressions
   before they reach production.

3. E2E TESTS (T15.3, target 30+):
   Functional tests for integrated features. Run via ftui-harness in headless mode.
   Test scenarios: chart rendering, tree navigation, clipboard, export, theme switching,
   form submission, error boundary recovery.

CI INTEGRATION:
- All tests run with AM_TUI_EFFECTS=false and AM_TUI_AMBIENT=off
- Performance benchmarks run in release mode
- E2E tests capture diagnostic artifacts on failure
- Test both 80x24 (minimum) and 200x50 (typical) terminal sizes

EXISTING TEST INFRASTRUCTURE:
- ftui-harness is already a workspace dependency (Cargo.toml line 49)
- tests/e2e/ directory has 37+ test scripts
- tests/e2e/test_tui_v2.sh exists (from br-2bbt.11.2)
- Create test_tui_v3.sh alongside it","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:12:23Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-323e","title":"acknowledge_message returns now instead of actual DB timestamps on idempotent calls","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-09T17:35:53.704837286Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T17:42:51.875887596Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T17:42:51.875867318Z","close_reason":"Fixed mark_message_read and acknowledge_message to read back actual timestamps after COALESCE UPDATE","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-32ax","title":"T2.5: Wire am flake-triage CLI subcommand (scan/reproduce/detect subcommands)","description":"## Objective
Expose flake triage features through a cohesive `am flake-triage` command surface.

## Work
- Wire `scan`, `reproduce`, and `detect` subcommands to native Rust implementations.
- Align flag semantics, output formatting, and exit behavior with CLI conventions.
- Ensure JSON mode is stable for automation and CI integrations.

## Deliverable
A complete native CLI interface for flake triage, replacing shell-first invocation patterns.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Implementation is native Rust and cross-platform, introducing no new operational dependency on external shell/Python tooling.
- Preserves required legacy behavior (or documented intentional changes) with deterministic CLI/API semantics and stable `--json` output where applicable.
- Emits structured diagnostics and runtime telemetry sufficient for user-facing troubleshooting and CI artifact capture.
- Has comprehensive test coverage tied to this behavior (unit + integration + e2e where relevant), including negative-path assertions.
- Demonstrates reproducible behavior under CI constraints (timeouts, retries, deterministic ordering/seed controls).
- Produces explicit logging output suitable for rapid root-cause analysis when failures occur.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:24:34.489039Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T05:00:33.521272766Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T05:00:23.446694541Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-32ax","depends_on_id":"br-154k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:26:17.498503833Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-32ax","depends_on_id":"br-1kk7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:26:17.697127473Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-32ax","depends_on_id":"br-36xx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:26:17.302231766Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":200,"issue_id":"br-32ax","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# T2.5: Wire am flake-triage CLI Subcommand

## What to build
Add a `FlakeTriage` variant to the CLI enum with three subcommands:
scan, reproduce, and detect.

## CLI interface
```
am flake-triage scan [--dir <path>] [--json]
    Walk directory for failure artifacts, summarize by test name

am flake-triage reproduce <artifact.json> [--verbose] [--json]
    Re-run the failed test from an artifact file

am flake-triage detect <test_name> [--seeds N] [--parallel] [--timeout 60s] [--json]
    Run test N times with different seeds to detect flakiness
```

## Implementation notes
- Add `FlakeTriage(FlakeTriageArgs)` to Cli enum in lib.rs
- FlakeTriageArgs has a subcommand enum: Scan, Reproduce, Detect
- Each subcommand maps to the corresponding function from T2.2/T2.3/T2.4
- --json flag produces structured JSON; without it, produce human-readable table output
- Default --dir for scan: tests/artifacts/ (common artifact location)
- Default --seeds for detect: 10

## Human-readable output examples
Scan:
```
Test                         Failures  Latest
test_concurrent_inbox        3         2026-02-10 14:23
test_cache_coherency         1         2026-02-09 08:41
```

Detect:
```
Flake detection: test_concurrent_inbox (10 seeds)
  Pass: 8/10  Fail: 2/10  Flake rate: 20%
  Failing seeds: 3, 7
  Verdict: FLAKY
```

## Location
crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/lib.rs (Cli enum addition)
crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/flake_triage.rs (run_flake_triage_command function)
","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:29:39Z"},{"id":363,"issue_id":"br-32ax","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented: FlakeTriageCommand enum with Scan/Reproduce/Detect variants. handle_flake_triage() wired into Commands enum and execute() dispatch. Scan walks dir tree for failure_context.json, supports --json. Reproduce reads artifact, runs repro via subprocess. Detect runs multi-seed subprocess runner with verdict output. All 44 core flake_triage tests pass, CLI compiles clean.","created_at":"2026-02-12T05:00:33Z"}]}
-{"id":"br-333hh","title":"T1.3: Integrate BarChart widget on ToolMetrics for latency distribution","description":"Add horizontal BarChart to the ToolMetrics screen showing P50/P95/P99 latency distributions
per tool. This replaces text-based latency display with a visual bar chart.

DESIGN:
- Each tool gets a row with three bars: P50 (chart_series_1), P95 (chart_series_2), P99 (chart_series_3)
- Bars are horizontal, labeled with millisecond values
- Sort tools by P99 descending (worst latency first)
- Color-code bars by severity: green < 100ms, yellow < 500ms, red >= 500ms
- Show up to 15 tools; use VirtualizedList if more

Use LatencyProvider from T1.1 to feed data.

FILES: tui_screens/tool_metrics.rs","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] BarChart renders showing P50/P95/P99 per tool
- [ ] Bars color-coded by latency severity
- [ ] Sorted by P99 descending
- [ ] Uses theme palette chart_series colors
- [ ] Shows tool name and ms value labels

Plan-space hardening additions:
- [ ] Comprehensive unit tests cover happy-path behavior, edge cases, invariants, and failure modes for this bead scope
- [ ] Integration/E2E (or PTY/stress where applicable) scripts validate end-user flows, degraded-mode behavior, and recovery paths for this bead
- [ ] Test and benchmark outputs include detailed structured diagnostics (scenario IDs, timings, error/reason codes, artifact paths, and repro commands)","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:07:31.109806210Z","updated_at":"2026-02-14T04:33:04.774311478Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["tool-metrics","tui","visualization"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-333hh","depends_on_id":"br-1k84y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:08:29.710964715Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-333hh","depends_on_id":"br-rk4gw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:08:07.967180807Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

All 29 tool_metrics tests pass, clippy+fmt clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["tool-metrics","tui","visualization"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-333hh","depends_on_id":"br-1k84y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:08:29.710964715Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-333hh","depends_on_id":"br-rk4gw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:08:07.967180807Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-33dh","title":"T5.5: Tests for port detection and token loading edge cases","description":"## Objective
Create targeted unit/integration coverage for serve startup edge cases around port detection, reuse, and token loading.

## Work
- Test bind-conflict scenarios, reuse decision branches, and environment precedence behavior.
- Validate auth/token startup decisions, including missing token and `--no-auth` paths.
- Assert high-signal diagnostics and stable exit/report behavior under failure.

## Deliverable
A regression-resistant test suite for native serve startup ergonomics.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Includes comprehensive unit, integration, and end-to-end coverage for this task's behavior (happy path, failure path, and boundary conditions).
- Produces detailed, machine-readable run artifacts: structured JSON summary, per-step command traces, stdout/stderr capture, timing data, and environment metadata needed for deterministic reproduction.
- Logging is high-signal and explicit: each assertion failure reports scenario ID, input fixture, expected vs actual values, and a recommended next debug step.
- CI execution is deterministic (seed/time controls, explicit timeouts/retries, no hidden external dependencies) and failures are actionable without manual log spelunking.
- Results are wired into standard quality gates so regressions block merges.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PearlTower","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:25:08.408664554Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T09:18:56.477220954Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-33dh","depends_on_id":"br-3534","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:26:27.483851328Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-33dh","depends_on_id":"br-3tva","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:26:27.285469460Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":221,"issue_id":"br-33dh","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# T5.5: Tests for Port Detection and Token Loading

## What to test

### Port detection tests
1. **Free port**: Bind to port 0 (OS-assigned), verify check_port returns Free
2. **Occupied port**: Bind a TcpListener, verify check_port returns OtherProcess or
   can detect it's in use
3. **Health check identification**: Start a minimal HTTP server that responds to
   /mcp/health, verify check_port identifies it as AgentMailServer

### Token loading tests
4. **Basic .env parsing**: File with `HTTP_BEARER_TOKEN=mytoken` → returns "mytoken"
5. **Quoted values**: Double quotes, single quotes → stripped correctly
6. **Whitespace handling**: Leading/trailing whitespace on key and value → trimmed
7. **Comments**: Lines starting with # → skipped
8. **Empty lines**: → skipped
9. **Multiple entries**: Last value wins (matching grep | tail -n1 behavior)
10. **Missing file**: → returns None (not an error)
11. **Empty token**: `HTTP_BEARER_TOKEN=` → returns None (empty string treated as absent)
12. **Env var takes precedence**: Set env var, also have .env file → env var wins
13. **Path fallback**: preferred path missing, legacy path exists → uses legacy

### Integration tests
14. **--no-auth flag**: Verify token is not loaded even if .env exists
15. **--env-file flag**: Verify custom path is used

## Location
crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/startup_checks.rs (mod tests) or new test file
","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:34:01Z"},{"id":405,"issue_id":"br-33dh","author":"PearlTower","text":"Implemented edge-case tests for this track: (1) added startup probe test in crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/startup_checks.rs verifying check_port_status detects an Agent Mail server via /health response signature; (2) expanded serve token-loading/reuse tests in crates/mcp-agent-mail/src/main.rs covering custom --env-file loading, non-override when token already set, quoted/export/whitespace parsing, last-value-wins behavior, empty-last-value handling, and missing-file handling. Validation attempt was blocked by upstream compile break outside this repo diff: /dp/frankensqlite/crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/engine.rs unresolved import fsqlite_mvcc::PageData + to_vec method mismatch. This currently prevents running cargo test for affected crates in this workspace session.","created_at":"2026-02-12T09:17:32Z"},{"id":407,"issue_id":"br-33dh","author":"PearlTower","text":"Re-validated just now: cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail still blocked by upstream /dp/frankensqlite break (fsqlite-core src/connection.rs uses PageData::as_slice which no longer exists). This is outside the touched files in this bead; keeping br-33dh in_progress until upstream dependency compiles again so tests can be executed.","created_at":"2026-02-12T09:18:56Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-33ha","title":"Comprehensive test coverage hardening (unit + E2E)","description":"Comprehensive test coverage hardening for the mcp-agent-mail-rust workspace.

## Current State (post-audit)
- 2,263+ workspace tests, 0 failures
- Server/TUI unit tests: DONE (Tracks 1-2 closed, ~900+ tests added)
- E2E suites: 28+ scripts with 135+ test cases

## Remaining Work (9 task beads across 4 tracks)

### Track 3: HTTP-level E2E (br-33ha.4) — 2 beads
- Health endpoints (br-33ha.4.3) — 10+ assertions
- CORS preflight (br-33ha.4.4) — 12+ assertions

### Track 4: Edge cases (br-33ha.5) — 1 bead
- DB corruption recovery (br-33ha.5.5) — 10+ assertions

### Track 5: Multi-agent production scenarios (br-33ha.6) — 3 beads
- Cross-project messaging (br-33ha.6.1) — 15+ assertions
- Concurrent agents (br-33ha.6.2) — 12+ assertions
- Contact policy enforcement (br-33ha.6.4) — 15+ assertions  ← NEW

### Track 6: Quality & observability (br-33ha.7) — 3 beads
- Logging audit (br-33ha.7.1) — audit ~28 suites
- JSON summary output (br-33ha.7.2) — CI integration [blocked by .7.1]
- Tool metrics E2E (br-33ha.7.3) — 10+ assertions

## Expected Totals When Complete
- ~85+ new E2E assertions across 6 new test scripts
- Logging improvements across ~28 existing suites
- JSON CI report infrastructure","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-09T03:14:19.386663950Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T04:39:54.749574466Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T04:39:54.749559628Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-33ha.1","title":"Add deterministic tests for doctor check beads issue-awareness probe","description":"Strengthen coverage for beads_rust integration by adding deterministic tests around doctor check beads_issue_awareness output and failure modes. Ensure check is present and statuses are stable when .beads is missing or available.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-09T03:28:02.340752591Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T03:30:47.708455037Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T03:30:47.708433958Z","close_reason":"Added deterministic test coverage for beads issue-awareness integration (missing .beads error path + seeded .beads count path), plus doctor check probe integration tests; full fmt/check/clippy pass","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-33ha.1","depends_on_id":"br-33ha","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-09T03:28:02.340752591Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
